data.title: Earnings Update: RaySearch Laboratories AB (publ) (STO:RAY B) Just Reported And Analysts Are Trimming Their Forecasts
data.description:
It's been a good week for RaySearch Laboratories AB (publ) (STO:RAY B) shareholders, because the company has just released its latest annual results, and the shares gained 3.6% to kr185. Results were roughly in line with estimates, with revenues of kr1.3b and statutory earnings per share of kr6.65. Earnings are an important time for investors, as they can track a company's performance, look at what the analysts are forecasting for next year, and see if there's been a change in sentiment towards the company. In the lead-up to this report, the analysts had been modelling revenues of kr1.50b and earnings per share (EPS) of kr8.92 in 2026. The analysts are less bullish than they were before these results, given the reduced revenue forecasts and the minor downgrade to earnings per share expectations.

Compare this against other companies (with analyst forecasts) in the industry, which are in aggregate expected to see revenue growth of 14% annually. So it's pretty clear that, while revenue growth is expected to slow down, the wider industry is also expected to grow faster than RaySearch Laboratories.

  • At a share price of €2.24 and trailing 12 month revenue of €1.23b, Eutelsat trades on a P/S of about 0.9x, compared with peers at 0.6x and the French Media industry at 0.5x, even though the supplied DCF fair value of €9.24 sits very far above the current price.
  • Bears argue that paying a premium P/S multiple for an unprofitable company is risky, and the data gives them some support, even with the low price against DCF:
    • Losses over the past five years have grown at roughly 67.7% per year and the company is expected to remain loss making over the next three years, which does not obviously line up with a higher multiple than peers.
    • The same dataset flags elevated share price volatility over the last three months, so anyone focusing on that 0.9x P/S versus lower peer multiples may see the DCF fair value gap as less convincing without clearer progress toward profitability.

For you as a shareholder or potential investor, the tension here is that the model based DCF fair value of €9.24 suggests a large upside gap, while the premium P/S and continuing losses give bears plenty to point at. 🐻 Eutelsat Communications Bear Case

Revenue Growth Forecast Trails French Market

  • The current dataset shows Eutelsat’s revenue forecast at about 4.6% annual growth over the next few years, compared with a 5.7% forecast for the wider French market, while EPS is expected to stay negative over that period.
  • Consensus style views often lean on LEO projects, government contracts and reduced GEO CapEx to support the long term case, and the numbers partly align but also leave questions:
    • Forecast revenue growth below the broader French market, despite all the LEO investment, means the growth profile is not especially fast compared with the benchmark that many investors use.
    • The fact that forecasts still show negative EPS over the next three years sits awkwardly with analyst targets that assume Eutelsat eventually reaches profit margins similar to the GB Media industry.

data.title: Riot Platforms Texas Tax Deal And AMD Lease Reframe Growth Story
data.description:
• Riot Platforms secured approval for a 10 year property tax abatement tied to its new Texas data center.
  • The company named Advanced Micro Devices as the first tenant at the facility, signaling early commercial traction.
  • The project is intended to support both data infrastructure services and institutional scale Bitcoin mining activity.

Riot Platforms, traded as NasdaqCM:RIOT, is pushing further into large scale infrastructure with this Texas build out while still tying the project to its core Bitcoin mining focus. Shares recently closed at $15.22, with a 7.5% return year to date and a 24.0% return over the past year, alongside a 131.0% return over three years and a 78.7% decline over five years. The mix of strong multi year gains and a weaker five year result highlights how volatile the stock has been for longer term holders.

The 10 year tax abatement and AMD tenancy provide a clearer view of how Riot is working to add another leg to its business beyond pure mining operations. For investors tracking NasdaqCM:RIOT, the key questions from here involve tenant demand, how the data center is utilized over time, and how this effort affects the balance between infrastructure revenue and Bitcoin mining exposure.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Riot Platforms by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Riot Platforms.

2 things going right for Riot Platforms that this headline doesn't cover.

This tax deal and the AMD lease point to Riot Platforms trying to turn its power capacity in Texas into a more diversified business, not just a pure Bitcoin miner. The 10 year property tax abatement could lower operating costs at the new site, which may help the economics of both data infrastructure services and mining. Landing AMD as the first tenant also gives Riot an anchor customer as it scales from 25 MW toward the planned 200 MW capacity, which can matter when competing with data center operators and miners such as Marathon Digital, CleanSpark, or larger cloud focused players.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Riot Platforms Narrative

  • The AMD tenancy directly addresses earlier concerns that Riot had yet to secure meaningful lease agreements and supports the narrative that its power assets can serve AI and high performance computing demand.
  • The long term commitment to a large Texas build out reinforces exposure to regional regulatory and energy market risks highlighted in the narrative, especially if grid conditions or legislation change.
  • The tax abatement itself is not fully captured in the broader story of power utilization and could influence future margin profiles at this site depending on how quickly capacity is filled.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Riot Platforms to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Analysts have flagged that reported earnings rely heavily on non cash items linked to Bitcoin holdings, so results can swing sharply with Bitcoin price moves even as data center activities ramp up.
  • ⚠️ Earnings are forecast to decline sharply over the next three years while Riot continues to invest in infrastructure, which could pressure sentiment if lease up of the Texas facility is slower than expected.
  • 🎁 Revenue is forecast to grow at a double digit rate each year, and the AMD agreement supports the idea that data center customers can be part of that growth rather than relying only on mining.
  • 🎁 Analysts have also highlighted very large earnings growth in the recent past, which, together with a 10 year tax abatement and power heavy assets, underpins interest in Riot as it competes for AI and Bitcoin related demand.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, you may want to watch how quickly Riot signs additional tenants beyond AMD, the pace of build out from 25 MW toward the planned 200 MW, and any updates on institutional scale Bitcoin mining capacity at the Texas site. Movements in analyst earnings estimates and ratings, which are currently supportive, will also matter as the company reports results that reflect both mining and data infrastructure revenue. Any changes in Texas regulation, power market conditions, or tax treatment around the abatement could also influence the long term economics of this project.

data.title: Safran Morocco Plant Links A320 Growth With Supply Resilience And Sustainability
data.description:
• Safran has opened a major landing gear production plant in Morocco focused on Airbus A320 programs.
  • The facility is designed to support global aerospace supply chains and use mainly decarbonized energy.
  • The project is expected to create hundreds of local jobs and expand Safran's manufacturing footprint.

Safran, listed as ENXTPA:SAF, is adding this Moroccan plant at a time when its shares trade around €332.8. The company has seen share price returns of 8.1% over the past week and 36.8% over the past year, as well as very large gains over 3 years and 5 years. For investors watching the civil aerospace segment, this additional capacity is directly tied to aircraft production programs such as the Airbus A320.

The move into Morocco gives Safran another production base that is closer to key European customers and supports supply continuity for landing gear systems. With the plant geared toward decarbonized energy use and job creation, the development also adds an environmental and social dimension that some investors may factor into their long-term view on ENXTPA:SAF.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Safran by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Safran.

We've flagged 1 risk for Safran. See which could impact your investment.

This Moroccan factory fits into Safran’s push to secure critical parts of its supply chain for narrowbody aircraft, particularly the Airbus A320 family, while tying production to lower carbon energy. For you as an investor, that links operational execution to topics like supply resilience, cost discipline, and customer stickiness with large airframers such as Airbus and Boeing, as well as competitors like Rolls-Royce in other product areas.

How This Fits Into The Safran Narrative

  • The new landing gear plant supports the narrative of long-term demand for narrowbody aircraft and associated aftermarket work, which underpins recurring revenue from Safran’s installed base.
  • It also adds execution risk around ramp-up, labor, and quality control that could challenge the thesis that Safran can continually expand capacity while keeping margins resilient.
  • The focus on 100% decarbonised energy and a larger industrial footprint in Morocco adds an environmental and geographic angle that is not fully captured in a story mainly centered on engines, acquisitions, and defense programs.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Concentration on programs like the Airbus A320 keeps Safran tied to production schedules and ordering patterns of a few major customers.
  • 🎁 A large, decarbonised production site that supports A320 output may strengthen Safran’s position in high-volume programs and support long-term services revenue on delivered equipment.
  • 🎁 Locating in a competitive aerospace cluster in Morocco could support cost efficiency and provide an additional buffer against supply disruptions in Europe.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, you may want to watch how quickly Safran brings the Moroccan plant to full capacity, and whether management links it directly to any changes in margin guidance or capital spending plans. Commentary around supply chain reliability, particularly for landing gear and narrowbody programs, will help you judge whether this move is easing constraints or adding new bottlenecks. data.title: StepStone Utmost Tie Up Expands UK Access To Evergreen Private Markets
data.description:
• StepStone Group (NasdaqGS:STEP) has partnered with Utmost to offer UK clients access to evergreen global private markets strategies.
  • The collaboration focuses on giving a wider range of UK investors exposure to diversified private markets portfolios.
  • The partnership aligns with government-backed efforts in the UK to broaden access to private markets investing.

For you as an investor, this move highlights how StepStone Group, a global private markets firm, is positioning its evergreen products for a broader UK audience. Evergreen structures, which are designed to provide ongoing access rather than fixed-life funds, have been drawing interest from investors looking for more flexible private markets exposure. The tie-up with Utmost points to growing demand for packaged solutions that can sit alongside traditional portfolios.

Looking ahead, this kind of partnership could influence how other managers approach distribution of private markets strategies in the UK. If investor interest in evergreen structures continues, you may see more collaborations between asset managers and insurance or platform providers, with StepStone’s agreement serving as an early example to watch.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for StepStone Group by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on StepStone Group.

We've flagged 3 risks for StepStone Group. See which could impact your investment.

For StepStone, the Utmost deal sits squarely in its push to grow private-wealth distribution, especially outside the US. Utmost gives the firm access to UK-based clients who are already using insurance-based wealth solutions, which can be a natural fit for evergreen private-markets products in Private Equity, Venture Capital & Growth, Private Credit, and Private Infrastructure. For you as an investor, this means more of StepStone’s future growth potential could come from recurring fee streams tied to diversified, long-term portfolios rather than one-off fundraising cycles. The partnership also lines up with UK policy efforts to channel more capital into unlisted assets, which may help uptake across a broader range of investors than traditional private-equity funds typically reach.

Advertisement

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ The company reported a net loss of US$123.45 million for the third quarter and US$528.02 million for the nine months ended 31 December 2025, so investors may want to watch how new partnerships translate into profitability over time.
  • ⚠️ Analysts have flagged 3 key risks for StepStone, including that the dividend is not well covered by earnings or free cash flows, which can limit flexibility if market conditions or fundraising slow.
  • 🎁 The Utmost partnership broadens access to StepStone’s evergreen private-markets range in the UK, which could help diversify its client base and fee sources compared with peers like Blackstone, KKR, or Apollo that also target wealth channels.
  • 🎁 Management has reported strong momentum in core fee-related earnings and a record year for fundraising, which provides some support for the firm’s push into international distribution and evergreen structures.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, it will be useful to track how quickly assets are raised through the Utmost channel, how that shows up in fee-related earnings, and whether StepStone can narrow its losses while maintaining the dividend. Comparing its progress with other private-markets managers that are expanding in wealth channels, such as Blackstone or KKR, can also help you gauge whether StepStone is gaining or giving up ground in this part of the industry.
